medications.indication_rationale: Controls histaminergic itch/swelling; source gave cetirizine 10mg stat then fexofenadine 180mg daily.
medications.dose_route_frequency: cetirizine 10mg or fexofenadine 180mg once daily (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Short course to the wheal suppresses local inflammation; source used betamethasone valerate 0.1% twice daily up to 5 days (a milder steroid such as hydrocortisone 1% is also reasonable).
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply thinly to the wheal twice daily for up to 5 days (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: oral/topical antibiotics for a fresh itchy bite
medications.indication_rationale: No infection — antibiotics are not indicated for an acute local reaction (only if secondary cellulitis develops).

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Apply a cold compress for ~15 minutes for itch; wash the area daily; do NOT scratch (prevents secondary infection).
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Cold relieves itch; scratching causes skin breaks and cellulitis risk.

patient_education_points.point: This is a big local reaction to an insect bite — your skin overreacted to the bite. It's not dangerous and settles over hours to a few days with an antihistamine and a steroid cream.
Use cold packs for the itch and try hard not to scratch — scratching can let bacteria in and cause a skin infection.

Call 000 immediately for any breathing difficulty, lip/tongue/throat swelling, whole-body hives or faintness. See a doctor over the next days if the redness spreads and becomes painful, a red streak travels up the leg, or you get a fever.

must_include_items: Cellulitis safety-net (spreading painful redness, streaking, fever over days)
acceptable_alternatives: Hydrocortisone 1% or a mid-potency steroid; any 2nd-gen antihistamine
errors_of_commission: Prescribing antibiotics for a fresh itchy bite
Diagnosing cellulitis on an hours-old itchy wheal
Sending a benign local reaction with a negative anaphylaxis screen to ED by ambulance
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires antihistamine + topical steroid, an anaphylaxis safety-net, and a (delayed) cellulitis safety-net.
